_doc: Master Reference change log. Newest first. 'note' shows in the tool's 'What's Changed Recently'. Add an optional 'bulletin' title to also post that entry to the CT-Resource-Page Live feed (run log-update.py).

entries.note: Finance-partner update and 2026 tax-credit note. Two emerging partners are marked Coming on the By Partner tab (not yet live, so do not quote them). EnFin (also written Infin or NFIN) is a solar-loan partner owned by Hanwha, the same parent as Qcells; solar loans only, soft credit pull, with a publicly stated minimum FICO around 600. We expect it to take a growing share of solar volume. GreenSky is a point-of-sale platform funded by partner banks, now owned by a Sixth Street-led consortium after Goldman Sachs sold it in March 2024; we intend it for roofing-only loans, prime-leaning credit around 650. A standalone battery lease or PPA (a battery-only third-party-owned option, which Palmetto LightReach markets) is also coming. 2026 tax-credit reality: the homeowner federal Section 25D credit ended after December 31, 2025, so an owned, loan, or cash solar system no longer gets the homeowner credit. In 2026 only a third-party-owned lease or PPA captures a 30 percent-style benefit, through the commercial 48E credit built into the rate.
entries.bulletin: Finance update: EnFin and GreenSky coming, battery-only lease coming, and the 2026 homeowner solar tax credit has ended

entries.note: LightReach policy updates (By Partner -> TPO): (1) Townhomes ARE allowed and do NOT need clear lines of delineation, even though LightReach's online docs say otherwise -- escalate any approval trouble to your manager. (2) Condos are not accepted, no exceptions. (3) Agriculture/Farm on the title is no longer accepted, even when only offsetting the main house on a residential meter. (4) Past-due utility balances up to $1,000 are approved at NTP but must be cleared before the Install milestone (Install Complete); tell the customer at the point of sale, and any past-due (even $1) needs a fresh bill showing a ZERO balance -- an actual bill, not a receipt or a website screenshot.
entries.bulletin: LightReach policy update: townhomes OK, condos & farm/ag not accepted, past-due bills must clear before install

entries.note: Cleaner page header: one title, a clear 'Current' back button, and the tabs on their own row. The CT Net Metering / SB4 card is now labeled and highlighted so it's easy to find.
Ground mounts are CASH ONLY -- no finance partner covers them. Flagged on every state's ground-mount rules and on the checklist.

CT page: added the Net Metering MOU (Senate Bill 4). From 2026-01-01, Netting-tariff customers pay a state-mandated, non-bypassable PURA charge ($0.0402/kWh produced to start), so they still get a monthly utility bill even when solar offsets usage. Not a Trinity fee; customer signs the CT Net Metering MOU.
entries.bulletin: CT Net Metering MOU (Senate Bill 4) -- customers still get a monthly bill

entries.note: CT page corrected: design to historical usage plus legislated EV/Heat Pump adders (incl. Ground Source Heat Pump +2,458 kWh); the 9 kW AC cap applies only to new builds / no-history homes; 5-year usage lookback added; EV/HP documentation is the CT MOU plus the adder noted in the combined packet. Battery: location is confirmed at the electrical site visit, not at point of sale.
entries.bulletin: CT guideline corrections: usage, EV/Heat Pump adders, battery

entries.note: Launched: Sales Operations Master Reference -- state sizing rules, finance-partner eligibility, the document checklist, and pricing in one place.
entries.bulletin: New tool: Sales Operations Master Reference

entries.note: Added a manager-only Contacts tab: ops by territory, finance milestones, sales escalations, partner support, and the approvals cheat sheet.
Checklist now opens in a simplified rep view, with a Full Ops toggle for the complete granular list.
Pricing: added a toggle to hide discontinued partners.
CT lease rates updated (LightReach): Eversource 22c (0% esc) / 18c (3% esc), UI 24c / 20c.
